title: Salmonid Mortality Analysis: TidyTuesday
text:
1 Overview
The Tidy Tuesday datasets this week (17/03/26) come from the suite of Salmonid mortality datasets published by the Norwegian Veterinary Institute. The Fish Health Report is the Norwegian Veterinary Institute’s annual status report on the health and welfare situation for Norwegian farmed fish and is based on official statistics, data from the Norwegian Veterinary Institute and private laboratories. The report also contains results from a survey among fish health personnel and inspectors from the Norwegian Food Safety Authority, as well as assessments of the situation, trends and risks.
This report contains uses two datasets (the monthly mortality data and the monthly loses data) to answer the following questions:

How does monthly mortality differ across the time period data is available for?
Which region has lowest mortality?
What other types of loses may be significant in addition to death of fish?

title: Salmonid Mortality Analysis: TidyTuesday
section: 2 How does monthly mortality differ over time?
text:
2 How does monthly mortality differ over time?
A summary of mortality statistics










2.1 Alternative interactive time-series plot


Code
#| label: mortality-plotly-ts
#| echo: true
#| message: false
#| warning: false


ts_plot_1 <- ggplot(mort_time, aes(date, median_mortality, color = species, group = species,
                          text = paste0("Species: ", species,
                                        "<br>Date: ", date,
                                        "<br>Median: ", round(median_mortality, 3)))) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(size = 1)
  labs(title = "Median mortality over time", x = NULL, y = "Median mortality") +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")


NULL


Code
ggplotly(ts_plot_1, tooltip = "text") |>
  style(mode = "lines+marker", line = list(width = 1.5)) |> 
  layout(
    showlegend = TRUE,
    xaxis = list(title = ""),
    yaxis = list(title = "Median Mortality"),
    legend = list(
      title = list(text = "Species"),
      orientation = "h",
      x = 0.5, xanchor = "center",
      y = -0.2, yanchor = "top"
    ),
    margin = list(b = 80)  #add bottom margin so the legend isn't clipped
  )


A marker object has been specified, but markers is not in the mode

Code
mort_season_base <- monthly_mortality_data |>
  mutate(
    date    = as.Date(date),
    year    = year(date),
    month   = factor(month(date, label = TRUE, abbr = TRUE), levels = month.abb),
    species = as.factor(species)
  ) |>
  filter(geo_group == "area") |>
  select(species, year, month, region, median)



Heatmaps (Year × Month)Boxplots (distribution by Month)



rainbowtroutsalmon




Code
sp <- "rainbowtrout"

hm <- mort_season_base |>
  filter(species == sp) |>
  group_by(year, month) |>
  summarise(value = median(median, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  complete(year, month)

hm_wide <- hm |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = month, values_from = value) |>
  arrange(year)

z <- as.matrix(select(hm_wide, -year))

plot_ly(
  x = colnames(z),
  y = hm_wide$year,
  z = z,
  type = "heatmap",
  colors = "Blues",
  hovertemplate = paste0(
    "Species: ", sp, "<br>",
    "Year: %{y}<br>",
    "Month: %{x}<br>",
    "Median mortality: %{z:.4f}<extra></extra>"
  )
) |>
  layout(
    title = paste0("Seasonality heatmap (", sp, "): Year × Month"),
    xaxis = list(title = ""),
    yaxis = list(title = ""),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 20, t = 60, b = 40)
  )










Code
sp <- "salmon"

hm <- mort_season_base |>
  filter(species == sp) |>
  group_by(year, month) |>
  summarise(value = median(median, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  complete(year, month)

hm_wide <- hm |>
  pivot_wider(names_from = month, values_from = value) |>
  arrange(year)

z <- as.matrix(select(hm_wide, -year))

plot_ly(
  x = colnames(z),
  y = hm_wide$year,
  z = z,
  type = "heatmap",
  colors = "Reds",
  hovertemplate = paste0(
    "Species: ", sp, "<br>",
    "Year: %{y}<br>",
    "Month: %{x}<br>",
    "Median mortality: %{z:.4f}<extra></extra>"
  )
) |>
  layout(
    title = paste0("Seasonality heatmap (", sp, "): Year × Month"),
    xaxis = list(title = ""),
    yaxis = list(title = ""),
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 20, t = 60, b = 40)
  )












rainbowtroutsalmon




Code
sp <- "rainbowtrout"

df <- mort_season_base |>
  filter(species == sp)

plot_ly(
  data = df,
  x = ~month,
  y = ~median,
  type = "box",
  boxpoints = "outliers",
  jitter = 0.25,
  pointpos = -1.6,
  color = I("navyblue"),
  text = ~paste0(
    "Species: ", sp,
    "<br>Month: ", month,
    "<br>Year: ", year,
    "<br>County: ", region,
    "<br>Median mortality: ", round(median, 4)
  ),
  hoverinfo = "text"
) |>
  layout(
    title = paste0("Seasonality boxplots (", sp, "): distribution by month"),
    xaxis = list(title = ""),
    yaxis = list(title = "Median Mortality"),
    showlegend = FALSE,
    margin = list(l = 60, r = 20, t = 60, b = 40)
  )

df <- mort_season_base |>

plot_ly(

title: Salmonid Mortality Analysis: TidyTuesday
section: 3 Which region has the lowest mortality?
text:
3 Which region has the lowest mortality?
Average mortality for each region - overall lowest 15
Plotting mortality by region


Code
# Filtering for just salmon
#salmon_lowest_region <- lowest_regions |> 
#  filter(species == "salmon")

# Filtering for just rainbow trout
#trout_lowest_region <- lowest_regions |> 
#  filter(species == "rainbowtrout")

# Salmon lowest mortality graph
ggplot(lowest_regions,
       aes(x = reorder(region, avg_median_mortality),
           y = avg_median_mortality,
           fill = species)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Counties with Lowest Average Median Mortality",
    subtitle = "Filtered to geo_group = 'County'",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Average median mortality"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Salmonid Mortality Analysis: TidyTuesday
section: 4 What other types of losses are significant?
text:
4 What other types of losses are significant?
The losses dataset includes:

dead
discarded
escaped
other

We reshape the dataset to explore proportions.
Summarise total losses by type


Code
# For each loss type, compute total and mean share.
loss_summary_table<- loss_long |>
  group_by(loss_type) |>
  summarise(
    total_count = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_share = mean(share, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  arrange(desc(total_count))

loss_summary_table <- loss_summary_table %>%
  rename(
    "Loss Type" = loss_type,
    "Total Count" = total_count,
    "Average Share" = avg_share
  )

# Step 3 — produce a styled GT table
loss_summary_table %>%
  gt() %>%
  
  # Add a title and subtitle
  tab_header(
    title = "Summary of Loss Types",
    subtitle = "Total losses and their average proportion"
  ) %>%
  
  # Format numbers nicely
  fmt_number(
    columns = "Total Count",
    sep_mark = ",", 
    decimals = 0
  ) %>%
  fmt_percent(
    columns = "Average Share",
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%

  # Highlight rows (example: highlight the largest total count)
  data_color(
    columns = "Total Count",
    colors = scales::col_numeric(
      palette = c("#D9EAF7", "#004F9F"),  # light blue → NHS blue
      domain = NULL
    )
  ) %>%

  # Add borders around table + row separators
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "all",
      color = "grey60",
      weight = px(1)
    ),
    locations = cells_body()
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_borders(
      sides = "bottom",
      color = "black",
      weight = px(2)
    ),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  ) %>%

  # Style column headers (remove underscores, nicer background)
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#E5F0FA"),       # NHS light blue background
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
    ),
    locations = cells_column_labels()
  )








Summary of Loss Types


Total losses and their average proportion

Loss over time


Code
# Summarise total losses by type per month.
loss_time <- loss_long |>
  group_by(loss_type, date) |>
  summarise(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(loss_time,
       aes(x = date, y = total, colour = loss_type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Loss Types Over Time",
    subtitle = "Aggregated across all regions and species",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Number of fish"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +  # prettier y-axis
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")









Loss composition by region


Code
# Compute regional loss composition (share of each type)
loss_by_region <- loss_long |>
  filter(geo_group == "county") |> 
  group_by(region, loss_type) |>
  summarise(total = sum(count, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  group_by(region) |>
  mutate(share = total / sum(total)) |>
  ungroup()

# Select the 5 regions with the highest total losses.
top_regions <- loss_by_region |>
  group_by(region) |>
  summarise(total_losses = sum(total), .groups = "drop") |>
  arrange(desc(total_losses)) |>
  slice_head(n = 5) |>
  pull(region)

# Plot the composition.
loss_by_region |>
  filter(region %in% top_regions) |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(region, share), y = share, fill = loss_type)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Loss Composition by Region",
    subtitle = "Top 5 counties by total losses",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Share of total losses"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
